steps.title: Clear fractions to get a quadratic
steps.content: Multiply every term by $148cx$: $$148 = cx^{2} + 148x \implies cx^{2} + 148x - 148 = 0.$$

steps.title: Solve the discriminant equation
steps.content: Exactly one solution means the quadratic's discriminant is $0$. For $cx^2+148x-148=0$, the discriminant is $$148^2-4(c)(-148)=148^2+4c(148).$$ Set the discriminant equal to $0$ and solve to get $c=-37$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Solving the same equation by hand: $$148^2+592c=0$$ $$21{,}904+592c=0$$ $$c=-37.$$ The answer is -37.
